title: Evening Wrap: ASX 200 hits fresh highs as TLS jumps 3.6% on dividend and buyback, ZIP crashes -34% on result, WDS +4.4%
content:
The S&P/ASX 200 closed 79.2 points higher, up 0.88%.

ASX 200 logged a fourth straight gain, with eight of the eleven major sectors higher, led by energy and telecommunications. Reporting season beats also supported a broad risk-on session.

In economic news, stronger jobs data reinforced expectations for another rate hike.

Let's dive in!

Today in Review
Thu 19 Feb 26, 5:33pm (AEST)

Markets
ASX 200 Session Chart

The S&P/ASX 200 (XJO) finished 79.2 points higher at 9,086.2, 0.88% from its session low and 0.35% from its high. In the broader-based S&P/ASX 300 (XKO) advancers beat decliners by a solid 180 to 94.

Energy (XEJ) (+3.8%) surged on oil’s sharp overnight jump, led by Woodside Energy (WDS) (+4.4%), while Santos (STO) (+5.5%) rallied after its FY25 result.

Consumer Discretionary (XDJ) (-2.9%) lagged after Wesfarmers (WES) (-5.6%) fell as its second-half sales growth missed expectations, while Lovisa (LOV) (-12.8%) dived on a weaker-than-expected half-year result.

In stock specific news:

Zip (ZIP) (-34%) cratered after a half-year result miss and softer US momentum.

Telstra (TLS) (+3.6%) hit all-time highs after its dividend beat expectations and it upsized its buyback.

Sonic Healthcare (SHL) (+9.8%) surged after beating expectations and re-affirming guidance.

Hub24 (HUB) (+14.1%) jumped on a strong earnings result.

In Asian trade, spot gold (+0.1%) was little changed at around US$4,990/oz, but gold miners traded higher, led by Northern Star Resources (NST) (+1.2%) and Evolution Mining (EVN) (+2.4%).

Be sure to click/scroll through for the usual reporting of the major sector and stock-specific moves, the broker responses to them, as well as all the key economic data in tonight's Evening Wrap.

ChartWatch will be back tomorrow... I did ask Carl if he had any advice regarding last night's Comp candle, and today's ASX 200 candle, and he only had this to say: "D vs S = P — they know what to do!"

summary:
• The ASX 200 closed higher, up 0.88%, marking its fourth consecutive day of gains.
• The Energy sector was a strong performer, surging 3.80%, while Consumer Discretionary lagged, down 2.99%.
• Key individual stock movers included Telstra (TLS) hitting all-time highs and Zip (ZIP) crashing significantly on its results.
• Stronger jobs data reinforced expectations for a potential interest rate hike.

🎯 My Portfolio:
• ZIP - Crashed 34% following a half-year result miss and softer US momentum.

📈 Stocks to Watch (Bullish):
• TLS - Hit all-time highs, boosted by a better-than-expected dividend and an upsized buyback.
• HUB - Jumped 14.1% on the back of a strong earnings result.
• SHL - Surged 9.8% after exceeding expectations and re-affirming guidance.

📉 Stocks to Watch (Bearish):
• WES - Fell 5.6% as its second-half sales growth missed expectations.
• LOV - Dived 12.8% on a weaker-than-expected half-year result.
• MPL - Fell 5.6% following its HY26 Results.
